qa_pairs.question: Who should make a request for Compute Canada support regarding COVID-19 research, and what basic information should always be included?
qa_pairs.answer: The Principal Investigator (PI) should be the one making the request. Researchers should always include a short description of the research, a justification of how it will help solve the current crisis, and a description of the type of support needed.

qa_pairs.question: What specific details are required when applying for a short-term allocation of advanced research computing resources for COVID-19 research from Compute Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: When applying for a short-term allocation, researchers must provide a description of the proposed COVID-19 research (including the specific problem and why advanced resources are critical), details of any awarded funding for COVID-19 research (agency, amount, and portion for computational research), expected research outcomes, start and end dates, requested resources (number of CPU cores, GPUs, and amount of storage for /project and/or /nearline spaces), a preferred system, a justification for the resources and system selected, and any software, security, or other special requirements.
